message:
Thread bound parameters through subquery rewriting pipeline

Previously, the subquery rewriting functions (rewrite_subquery_statement,
rewrite_subqueries, rewrite_in_subqueries_select, rewrite_in_expr,
rewrite_in_select_core) all passed Some(&[]) when executing materialized
subqueries, discarding any user-supplied bound parameters. This caused
IN-subquery expressions like "SELECT 5 IN (SELECT ?1)" to fail when
parameters were provided.

- Add `params: Option<&[SqliteValue]>` parameter to the entire rewriting
  pipeline so bound parameters propagate to subquery execution
- Pass actual params (not empty slice) when rewriting IN/EXISTS/scalar
  subqueries in fallback paths (GROUP BY, JOIN, expression-only SELECT)
- Skip WAL checkpoint on close when journal_mode is Delete, since there
  is no WAL file to checkpoint in that mode
- Add test for expression SELECT with IN subquery using bound parameter
- Add test confirming close succeeds in delete journal mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
